heading: Status
label: 7251 Status
text: There is hereby established, under Article I of the Constitution of the United States, a court of record to be known as the United States Court of Appeals for Veterans Claims.
notes: Amendments1998— substituted “Court of Appeals for Veterans Claims” for “Court of Veterans Appeals”.Pub. L. 105–3681991— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 102–40section 4051 of this title

Change of Name, , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–368, title V, § 511(a)112 Stat. 3341Nov. 11, 1998“The United States Court of Veterans Appeals is hereby renamed as, and shall hereafter be known and designated as, the United States Court of Appeals for Veterans Claims.”, , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–368, title V, § 512(c)112 Stat. 3342Nov. 11, 1998“Any reference in a law, regulation, document, paper, or other record of the United States to the United States Court of Veterans Appeals shall be deemed to be a reference to the United States Court of Appeals for Veterans Claims.”

Effective Date of 2001 Amendment; Construction, (d), , , provided that:Pub. L. 107–103, title VI, § 603(c)115 Stat. 999Dec. 27, 2001“(c).—ConstructionThe repeal in subsection (a) [repealing , formerly set out as a note below] may not be construed to confer upon the United States Court of Appeals for Veterans Claims jurisdiction over any appeal or other matter not within the jurisdiction of the Court as provided in , United States Code.section 402 of Pub. L. 100–687section 7266(a) of title 38“(d).—ApplicabilityThe repeals made by subsections (a) and (b) [repealing , formerly set out as a note below, and , formerly set out as a note under ] shall apply to any appeal filed with the United States Court of Appeals for Veterans Claims—section 402 of Pub. L. 100–687section 403 of Pub. L. 100–687section 5904 of this title“(1)on or after the date of the enactment of this Act []; orDec. 27, 2001“(2)before the date of the enactment of this Act but in which a final decision has not been made under , United States Code, as of that date.”section 7291 of title 38

Effective Date of 1998 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–368, title V, § 513112 Stat. 3342Nov. 11, 1998“This subtitle [subtitle B (§§ 511–513) of title V of , see Tables for classification], and the amendments made by this subtitle, shall take effect on the first day of the first month beginning more than 90 days after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”Pub. L. 105–368Nov. 11, 1998

Effective Date, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 100–687, div. A, title IV, § 401102 Stat. 4122Pub. L. 101–94, title III, § 301103 Stat. 628Nov. 18, 1988Aug. 16, 1989“(a).—General Effective DateExcept as otherwise provided in this section, this division (and the amendments made by this Act) [div. A (§§ 1–403) of , known as the ‘Veterans’ Judicial Review Act’, see Tables for classification] shall take effect on .Pub. L. 100–687September 1, 1989“(b).—Effective Date for Certain Transition ProvisionsThe amendment made by section 201(a) [amending section 4001 [now 7101] of this title] shall take effect on .February 1, 1989“(c).—Date of EnactmentSections 201 (other than subsection (a)), 208, 209, 302, and 303, and the amendments made by those sections [see Tables for classification], shall take effect on the date of the enactment of this Act [].Nov. 18, 1988“(d).—Board of Veterans’ AppealsSections 202, 203, 205, 206, and 207 [see Tables for classification] shall take effect as of . Section 204 [amending section 4004 [now 7104] of this title] shall take effect on .January 1, 1989September 1, 1989“(e).—Commencement of Operation of Court of Veterans AppealsNotwithstanding subsection (a), the United States Court of Veterans Appeals [now United States Court of Appeals for Veterans Claims] established pursuant to chapter 72 of title 38, United States Code (as added by section 301) shall not begin to operate until at least three judges have been appointed to the court.”

Chapter Applicable to Claims Alleging Previous Determination the Product of Clear and Unmistakable Error, , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–111, § 1(c)(2)111 Stat. 2272Nov. 21, 1997“Notwithstanding section 402 of the Veterans Judicial Review Act ( note), chapter 72 of title 38, United States Code, shall apply with respect to any decision of the Board of Veterans’ Appeals on a claim alleging that a previous determination of the Board was the product of clear and unmistakable error if that claim is filed after, or was pending before the Department of Veterans Affairs, the Court of Veterans Appeals [now Court of Appeals for Veterans Claims], the Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit, or the Supreme Court on the date of the enactment of this Act [].”38 U.S.C. 7251Nov. 21, 1997

Chapter Applicable to Cases Filed On or AfterNovember 18, 1988, , , as amended by , , , which provided that this chapter applied to any case in which a notice of disagreement was filed under on or after , was repealed by , , .Pub.
